PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE WHO ARE THE PARTIES IN THE ALLEGED CONTRACT. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR "GENERAL NEGLIGENCE" IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS CANNOT STATE A NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION ON BEHALF OF THEIR DAUGHTER, AND THERE APPEARS TO BE NO REASONABLE LIKELIHOOD THAT PLAINTIFFS CAN AMEND TO CURE THE DEFECTS THAT APPEAR IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AS TO "GENERAL NEGLIGENCE." DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NICOLE COLLINS, PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER.
JOANNA L. STOREY, ESQ. & AND ROBERT S. WILLOUGHBY, ESQ., 415.288.9800, FOR DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 75 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have leave to seek appointment as guardian ad litem and plead a claim for medical malpractice, breach of contract, and/or fraud or concealment on behalf of their daughter, if they can allege facts in good faith to support those claims and as set forth below. The demurrer to the intentional tort cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead fraud or concealment with more specificity. Plaintiff fails to cite facts showing fraud or concealment. The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff fails to allege an express contract promising a particular result. See Depenbrok v. Kaiser (1978) 79 Cal.App.3d 167, 170-171. Further, Plaintiffs do not have standing to assert this breach of contract claim as parents; it must be asserted as guardian ad litem of the child. The Court rejects Defendant's res judicata/collateral estoppel argument. The federal court dismissed the federal action based on Eleventh Amendment immunity and Defendant fails to show that the ruling was on the merits or that Defendant's alleged malpractice was actually litigated. plaintiff has leave to amend, if she has good faith, a cause of action against defendant. 1 Order for both motions on calendar today in this case signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling

JOANNA L. STOREY, ESQ. & AND ROBERT S. WILLOUGHBY, ESQ., 415.288.9800, FOR DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINTis denied as moot. Plaintiff has leave to amend in conformance with the Court's ruling on the companion demurrer. 1 Order for both motions on calendar today in this case signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DERRICK WHITE, TEL. NO. 374-4216, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. JOANNA L. STOREY OF HASSARD BONNINGTON, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the first cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with 30 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege who are the parties in the alleged contract. The demurrer to the first cause of action for "general negligence" is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs cannot state a negligence cause of action on behalf of their daughter, and there appears to be no reasonable likelihood that Plaintiffs can amend to cure the defects that appear in the Second Amended Complaint as to "general negligence." Amended complaint to be filed by January 30, 2013. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077
